mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins and cause rapid negative S&P 500 price adjustments within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during INDPRO contractions exceeding 1.0% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 3.5% signal persistent price pressures that force equity markets to discount future earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL crosses the 0.3% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over 60-day horizons in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth revisions above 2.0% confirm robust aggregate demand that reinforces corporate earnings trajectories and produces positive S&P 500 returns within one week of release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive directional bias following GDPC1 prints above 2.0% annualized growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in industrial production signals a manufacturing downturn that reduces capital expenditure expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive during 3-month INDPRO declines, or the signal loses predictive power in late-cycle regimes.
